__instructions: Copy this file, rename it to your-scale-name.json, and fill in the fields. Delete all __note keys before saving.
__id_note: Must exactly match the filename without .json. Use lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens.

name.en: Full Name of the Scale in English

__abbreviation_note: Short acronym for the scale (e.g. 'ASES'). Leave empty string if none.
__role_note: Your relationship to this scale. One of: 'developed' (you created it from scratch), 'translated' (you translated an existing scale), 'adapted' (you culturally adapted an existing scale).
__construct_note: Short label for the construct being measured (e.g. 'Academic Self-Efficacy'). Shown on the scale card.
__year_note: Year the scale was developed or your adaptation was published.
__original_authors_note: For translated/adapted scales: list the original authors as ['Lastname, Firstname']. Leave as empty array [] for scales you developed.
__adaptation_year_note: For translated/adapted scales: year of your adaptation/translation. Leave null if not applicable.

description.en: A brief description of what this scale measures, who it is for, and its main use cases.
description.tr: Bu ölçeğin ne ölçtüğüne, kime yönelik olduğuna ve temel kullanım alanlarına ilişkin kısa bir açıklama.

__item_count_note: Total number of items (questions) in the scale as an integer.

description.en: Brief description of what this subscale measures.
description.tr: Bu alt ölçeğin ne ölçtüğünün kısa açıklaması.

description.en: Brief description of what this subscale measures.
description.tr: Bu alt ölçeğin ne ölçtüğünün kısa açıklaması.

__subscales_note: Array of subscale objects. Each must have 'name' (string matching the key in cronbach_alpha_subscales), 'item_count' (integer), and 'description' (bilingual). Leave as empty array [] if the scale has no subscales.
__response_format_note: Describe the response options. E.g. '7-point Likert', 'Yes/No', 'Frequency scale (0-3)'.
__target_population_note: Who the scale is designed to be used with.
__languages_available_note: Array of ISO 639-1 language codes for available translations.

reliability.__subscale_alpha_note: Keys must exactly match the 'name' field in each subscale object. Remove this key.

validity_notes.en: Brief description of validity evidence: factor structure, convergent/discriminant validity, sample characteristics, etc.
validity_notes.tr: Geçerlilik kanıtlarının kısa açıklaması: faktör yapısı, yakınsak/ayrışan geçerlilik, örneklem özellikleri vb.

citation.en: Lastname, F. (2024). Full scale name [Abbreviation]. Journal Name, 10(2), 1-20. https://doi.org/10.xxxx/xxxx
citation.tr: Soyadı, A. (2024). Ölçeğin tam adı [Kısaltma]. Dergi Adı, 10(2), 1-20. https://doi.org/10.xxxx/xxxx

__citation_note: APA-format citation string for how others should cite this scale.
__original_citation_note: For translated/adapted scales only: APA-format citation for the original scale. This appears as a second citation box on the detail page under 'Citation for Original Scale'. Leave empty strings for scales you developed.
__reference_publication_note: The 'id' of the publication JSON where this scale was originally published/validated. Leave empty string if not in your publications list.
__featured_note: Set to true to feature this scale at the top of the scales list.
